name: scheme
short_description: Bash script to dynamically modify Neovim colorscheme and background settings from the command line.
long_description: A lightweight bash utility that allows developers to quickly change their Neovim editor's colorscheme and background (light/dark) settings without manually editing the configuration file. The script modifies the init.vim file directly using sed, supporting separate or combined colorscheme and background changes. It's designed for developers who want to switch editor themes from the command line, making theme switching as simple as running a single command with optional parameters.

modules.description: Main script that parses command-line arguments and modifies Neovim init.vim configuration to change colorscheme and background settings using sed.
Installation script that copies scheme.sh to /usr/local/bin and makes it executable, with backup functionality for existing installations.
